source: Learning Ansible with Rocky - Working With Jinja Templates: Adding variables to a configuration file with the template module

questions.q: Which module renders a Jinja template into a file on the target?
questions.why: The template module renders a .j2 template to the destination path.

questions.q: In a Jinja template, how is a variable placeholder written?
Which two template-task parameters set the template file and the output path?
questions.why: src is the .j2 template; dest is the generated file.

questions.q: How can variables be supplied to a template task?
options: From the playbook/task vars or inventory (and external files)
questions.why: Variables defined in the playbook, task vars, or inventory (or external files) are injected into the template.

questions.q: The template module is idempotent. What does that mean here?
options: The file is (re)written only when the rendered content actually changes
questions.why: Idempotent execution means the template is applied only if changes are detected.

questions.q: Run the playbook your_playbook.yml to apply the template.
questions.why: ansible-playbook <file> applies the play, rendering the template to the target.
